title: 7 Dividend Stocks to Buy (and Never Sell)
text: When it comes to finding the best long-term dividend stocks to buy, yield should one be a key part of your overall criteria. It may sound tempting to build a dividend portfolio made up entirely of high-yielding names, but there are some flaws to such a strategy.

title: 3 Semiconductor Stocks to Buy (and Never Sell)
text: Recently, semiconductor stocks surged dramatically. Outstanding earnings and promising forecasts from Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA ) sparked this rally, leading to a stellar week for the sector.

title: Why TSMC, Texas Instruments, and Qualcomm Stocks Popped
text: Powerful predictions of profits from artificial intelligence sales drove Nvidia and Marvell shares higher this week. On Friday, the rest of the sector is joining in as investors bet on cheaper ways to invest in AI.

title: Tech Dividends Done Right: Texas Instruments
text: Texas Instruments is a unique dividend growth stock in the technology sector, offering a 3.0% yield and consistent dividend hikes. The company's efficient operations and investments position it well for the future, despite near-term headwinds in chip demand.

title: Texas Instruments Will Benefit From the Artificial Intelligence Boom
text: Texas Instruments ( TXN , Financial) has been underperforming the Nasdaq Composite year-to-date with gains of nearly 3% versus the index's gains of 20%. Does this huge gap signal potential problems for this technology company?

title: 3 Chip Stocks Suited Nicely for Income Investors
text: Semiconductors are back in style in 2023 after a harsh 2022, with many delivering positive returns year-to-date. And for those with an appetite for income, these three could be worth your attention.

title: 3 Semiconductor Stocks Underperforming Now but Poised for Future Growth
text: These three chip stocks look bleak in the short term, but could this be a buying opportunity for long-term investors?

title: TI CEO Haviv Ilan to speak at Bernstein investor conference
text: DALLAS , May 10, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Texas Instruments Incorporated (TI) (Nasdaq: TXN) President and Chief Executive Officer Haviv Ilan will speak at the Bernstein 39th Annual Strategic Decisions Conference in New York City on Thursday, June 1, at 10 a.m. Eastern time.

title: Don't Miss Out. 7 Legacy Tech Stocks Primed for a Comeback.
text: The market is full of legacy technology companies whose stocks have fallen on hard times. Some of these companies have been around for nearly 100 years and are well-known household names.

title: The 7 Best Long-Term Dividend Stocks to Buy in May
text: Amidst the stock market landscape's choppiness, many investors seek stability by exploring dividend stocks to secure dependable returns. Both in the short term and the long run, stocks with average to above-average yields can return consistent gains, combining price appreciation and payouts.

title: Texas Instruments' Profit Tanked -- Time to Sell This Top Dividend Stock?
text: Texas Instruments is in the middle of a demand slump in the chip market, but that isn't the biggest risk facing the business. A period of extended spending is just beginning, which could impact TI's long-favored financial metric: free cash flow per share.

title: 3 Undervalued Semiconductor Stocks to Buy in May 2023
text: 2023 is an interesting time to be looking for undervalued semiconductor stocks. Valuations have come down for some firms, but that comes amid a major downturn in the earnings outlook for many of the industry's leading companies.

title: The 3 Best Blue-Chip Nasdaq Stocks to Buy Now
text: The Nasdaq-100 Index is generally considered the de facto proxy for Nasdaq-listed stocks. So it makes sense to pick from this index if you're looking for the best blue-chip Nasdaq stocks.

title: My Top 10 High Yield Dividend Stocks For May 2023
text: I present my top 10 High Yield dividend stocks that are poised to offer strong future returns. The annualized rate of return for this watchlist is 14.50% over the past 30 months.
